topServices.label: Amazon EC2
topServices.meta: Compute remains the largest driver; rightsizing idle non-prod instances is the fastest win.

topServices.label: Amazon RDS
topServices.meta: Storage and I/O rose after analytics workload expansion.

topServices.label: Amazon S3
topServices.meta: Tiering policy opportunity for backup and log buckets.

topServices.label: AWS Data Transfer
topServices.meta: Cross-region traffic continues to climb with customer growth.

topServices.label: Amazon EKS
topServices.meta: Cluster baseline stable; node pool utilization can improve.

environments.label: Development
environments.meta: Good candidate for scheduling and policy enforcement.

accounts.label: Data Platform
accounts.meta: Largest month-over-month increase due to analytics scale-up.

accounts.label: Shared Services
accounts.meta: Observability, identity, networking, and CI/CD foundations.

accounts.label: Sandbox
accounts.meta: Governance needed to cap experimentation costs.

insights.title: Spend is rising, but still inside budget
insights.body: Current forecast lands below budget, so the focus can stay on efficiency instead of emergency reduction.

insights.title: Data workloads are the main growth story
insights.body: RDS, S3, and transfer costs indicate analytics demand is driving the latest increase more than core application compute.

insights.title: Fastest savings are operational, not architectural
insights.body: Non-production scheduling, storage tiering, and savings plan expansion offer meaningful savings without delaying roadmap work.
